title: Tool makers, tool fitters and markers-out
description: Repair and open locks, make keys, change locks and safe combinations, and install and repair safes. Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, milling and engraving machines, and jig borers to make working models of metal or plastic objects. Includes template makers. Lay out, machine, fit, and assemble castings and parts to metal or plastic foundry patterns, core boxes, or match plates. (Based on 6 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Cut new or duplicate keys, using key cutting machines or impressions or code key machines.
Cut, shape, and form metal parts using lathes, power saws, and other tools.
Inspect and test products to verify conformance to specifications with precision measuring instruments.
Set up and operate machines such as lathes and drill presses to fabricate prototypes or models.
Study blueprints, drawings, and sketches to determine dimensions, equipment, and operation sequences.
Set up and maintain master key systems and keep records of company locks and keys.
Install alarm and electronic access systems, door hardware, and safes according to blueprints.
Disassemble mechanical or electrical locking devices and repair or replace worn parts using hand tools.
Fit and align fabricated parts to be welded or assembled and mark dimensions on workpieces.
Grind, file, and sand parts to finished dimensions, ensuring smooth and polished surfaces.
Lay out and fabricate metal structural parts and compute layout dimensions for further processing.
Repair and adjust safes and vault components, using hand tools and machining equipment.
Design and create templates, patterns, or core boxes based on work orders or sample parts.
Use computer-aided design (CAD) and computer-aided manufacturing (CAM) software to fabricate model parts.
Program computerized numerical control (CNC) machines to fabricate model parts and tools.
Record specifications and final dimensions of models for establishing operating standards and procedures.
Cut, shape and trim blanks or blocks to specified lengths or shapes using various power and hand tools.
Conduct test runs with tools or dies to ensure that parts meet specifications, making adjustments as necessary.
Repair and rework templates and patterns to ensure they meet required specifications.
